title: Celebrating 20 Wonderful Years at Teleperformance USA
body: Celebrating 20 Wonderful Years at Teleperformance USA
pros: As I reflect on reaching this 20-year milestone, I'm filed with gratitude. These two decades have been more than just a job - they've been a journey of growth, learning and shared purpose. When I started as part of the IT team, I never imagined how rewarding it would be to grow alongside this company. From planning and design to implementation and support, every phase has been an opportunity to contribute to something bigger. At the heart of it all has been a simple motivation: to help Teleperformance succeed and thrive. What has made these years truly special are the people. My colleagues have been endlessly supportive, helpful, and inspiring. Together, we've faced many challenges, celebrated victories, and built not just systems, but trust and camaraderie. Thank you Teleperformance for 20 years of opportunity, partnership, and purpose. Here's to the journey behind us, and the one still ahead. Blessings with tons of appreciation! Trix Lizardo
cons: My heart has always been helping Teleperformance grow. Of course, the journey wasn't without its hurdles. Keeping up with the tech waves in IT could feel like drinking from a firehose sometimes! But those very challenges are what taught me the value of an amazing team. My IT colleagues (Network, Telecom, Systems, Security, NOC, Desktop, BAs, AppDev) have been so supportive and helpful throughout. Together, we didn't just solve problems, we built the resilience that helps the company thrive. So thank you for 20 years of not just success but shared growth.

title: Not Worth the Stress/They Don't Care About You
body: Not Worth the Stress/They Don't Care About You
pros: Work from home-bonus so you don't actually have to look at management
cons: No work/life balance. They are good as long as you are killing yourself for them. As long as you are giving them 200%-no less, they are fine. This means, no family time, no time for your mental health. When life circumstances happen and you need to take time out for yourself, they abandon you and treat you like garbage. They will withhold your pay. My last day of work was October 1. I still have not received my final paycheck that should have came on October 22. They are telling me I have to wait until November 7 to receive it. Do not waste your time/energy on this job.

title: I completely agree with their Motto : We are a People Company
body: I completely agree with their Motto : We are a People Company
pros: As an Operations Customer Expert I, I have been given a clear pathway for professional growth. I've been able to expand my skill set through various upskilling programs, which has directly led to me taking on more complex tasks. I also appreciate the mentorship from my supervisor, who has helped me identify my career ambitions and chart a path for future advancement within the company. Teleperformance has demonstrated a strong commitment to employee well-being, and I feel I have a good work-life balance. The flexible scheduling has been excellent. The team environment is very collaborative and supportive; I feel comfortable approaching my team leader and colleagues for help. The leadership has been especially supportive, and I am consistently motivated by the positive and inclusive culture they foster.
cons: It's a really good place to start a career, it might appear new for some people but anyone can easily adapt to it.

title: Worked for TP on a licensed personal lines agent contract
body: Worked for TP on a licensed personal lines agent contract
pros: pay was decent, it was permanent, you could miss days as long as it wasnât a Saturday, easy to request time off.
cons: Working on weekends, heavy micromanagement, the job was too technical for the pay, bonuses were very low, the client didnât offer workers any discounts. Working on Saturday and Sunday was horrible. The shifts were 10 hours long on the weekend. Honestly wouldâve kept the job longer if it wasnât for the micromanaging. My manager would meet with me multiple times out the week to play back calls(good or bad) it was so cringy. The manager I had before didnât do all of that. So yeah I left. It wasnât horrible it depends on how much you can tolerate. It was starting to give me anxiety, the call volume was very low initially but it started to pick up.

title: Working at Teleperformance comes with several benefits
body: Working at Teleperformance comes with several benefits
pros: Working at Teleperformance comes with several benefits, especially for those seeking flexibility and comfort. Employees often have the option to work from home, allowing them to eliminate commutes. The company also offers flexible scheduling, making it easier to balance professional responsibilities with personal commitments. As a global organization, Teleperformance provides opportunities to grow within a diverse and inclusive culture, while offering access to training programs that support career development and skill enhancement.
cons: Working at Teleperformance comes with its challenges, such as certain projects having high call volumes, and performance is closely tracked with expectations of quality.

pros: Pros* The pros of working here at Teleperformance is that you You have room to grow- you have room to be yourself, and you also have room to Get to know others in your surroundings. I do appreciate this. and these are my pros.
cons: Cons* I have very few cons since I have been working here. As I've been working here. I do know that it is a very fast-moving pace. Job meaning things can change. process can change. Experience. And able to follow up on your own.

title: Mmm... I mean it's alright. I guess?

pros: - A facility to eat something inside - Pay is fine, but does not reflect my worth to this company - Some of the coworkers are like-minded, so easier to connect - Sometimes there will be a free meal
cons: - Pay does not increase, even if you have been with TP for a year. Sure, starting pay depends on each project, but TP is the one responsible for each of THEIR EMPLOYEE'S PAY - There are opportunities of growth, but at the major cost of your health, sanity, and energy - This company expects everything from you, but barely gives anything in return - The only true time that the company offers something of benefit to you is when their client visits - Barely recognizes the work you put into this company - Overall, this job does not care. Why would they? After all, it is a business that feels like a high school.

title: SME's need a raise
pros: Teleperformance is a great company to work for. I can work from the comfort of my own home and take care of my family. Also love that they offer overtime and benefits.
cons: I have been with the company going on 4 years and I am currently a SME for my team for almost two years. I understand that they took away atl's who where given a raise to help out TL's but now that they are gone that's more work on the SME and no pay raise seems outrageous. I love my position and handle it very well but as a SME we work along our Tl's to help the team keep up with at risks, audits, manager escalations and always asked to help with the queue. I believe we should have a raise just like those who were atl's had. Also I really hate that our PTO has change to only being issues after pay periods and not as a whole. This is really unfair to the employees that need their PTO and we shouldn't have to wait on it.

title: Where Growth Meets Great Reward
pros: Supportive Management: I appreciate the supportive nature of some managers, which fosters a positive work environment. Flexible Scheduling: The company often provides flexible work schedules, accommodating various personal commitments. Diverse Environment: Working with colleagues from all over the world enriches the workplace culture. Timely Compensation: Company consistently pays on time, without unpaid overtime. Entry-Level Opportunities: Teleperformance offers attractive starting pay for entry-level positions, making it accessible for many.
cons: Scheduling Issues: Weekly scheduling can be unpredictable, leading to challenges in personal planning. Limited Advancement: Opportunities for advancement may be limited in certain departments or locations.

title: great people terrible benifits
pros: The people you work alongside are truly outstandingâfellow employees consistently demonstrate collaboration, dedication, and a strong team spirit. However, interactions with some members of upper management can at times be challenging, which can impact overall communication and workplace harmony.
cons: While there are some genuinely good and supportive managers within the organization, they often lack the authority or influence needed to drive meaningful change. Unfortunately, upper-level management and the current benefits structure can be significant obstacles to making this a truly great place to work. Leadership at the highest levels needs to be more in touch with the day-to-day realities employees face and more committed to creating a positive, supportive work environment.

title: Teleperformance Cares
cons: I donât have a list of cons because, regardless of where you work, there will inevitably be tough days. However, if you take a moment to focus on the pros, youâll realize that even the most challenging or demanding days are worth it when you genuinely enjoy what you do. In the end, the positives are what truly matter.

title: Remote opportunity's and growth.
pros: I love being able to work remote and not having to worry about traffic/commuting. You are also able to move up quickly if you show up and want to grow with company. They also have trainings and learnings you can do to assist with personal and professional growth, There is a variety of different accounts you can work on if you don't like the campaign you are on you can always apply for another for lateral changes. There is also a good culture for dei/incultion which I appreciate.
cons: System issues, the computers are not always the best and can be frustrating. Compensation could be better for the work that you do. Not many paid holidays. Cost of living range.

title: You're just a butt in a seat
body: You're just a butt in a seat
pros: You can work from home.
cons: *Pay isn't great. *Leadership doesn't care. *Yearly raise is only for your current project, of you change projects you lose your annual raises. *Attendance policy is based on % of schedule adherence. You cant miss more than 3-4 hrs a week without being out of adherence. Meaning you cant even miss 1 full day due to illness without violating attendance policy. * They dont accept doctors notes to excuse absences. *Most projects have you working nights/weekends. Schedule swaps or changes rarely happen so if you get a crummy schedule, you'll probably be stuck with it forever or until you change projects. Next project probably also has crummy schedule. *If you get promoted to leadership and project ends, you wont always transfer you as leadership. You can be transferred back as agent at square 1. **But if remote is only option, if you can pass drug test /background check, they'll hire you.

title: Work From Home-Insurance
pros: * work from home * flexible on schedules with make up time sometimes available * licensing is paid for by the company, as well as any classes that are required. * PTO * Can do full or part time
cons: * have to take paid time off for every request off: this tells me the company does not want people to have vacations which is a HUGE red flag. Better way to avoid that is to give paid vacation that can only be used for vacation and sick time/appointment paid time off (or unpaid, that is fine too) can only be used that. * health benefits are terrible. Really expensive and high deductibles for "affordable" insurance, but unmanageable when you have dependents. If you need to use the insurance really only gives a discount. There is only one insurance that is listed with acceptable terms, but too expensive to take. I am forced to take this insurance since it is "affordable", but at least got my dependents a better insurance through someone else (I don't qualify). Maybe go through someone other than United Healthcare, they really do not care about their customer base.

cons: Itâs a call center like jobâ¦ itâs mind numbing. Work is easy, yes, but itâs a very negative environment (social media content AND on site). Employees are frequently angry, complaining, having inappropriate conversations for the work placeâ¦ sit on your butt for 8+ hours a day watching videosâ¦ itâs boring. Communication from higher ups (company level) is lacking. Sudden changes to scheduling, work flow, lay offs or hour cuts. The production floor is consistently freezing. title: MSS + iOS
pros: -We can work from home if your location allows it -Equipment is provided -If you meet your goals consistently you can move up quickly -You can get bonuses -Supervisor's get to plan their day how they want as long as you are getting your work done. -Advisor's get volunteered time off, and overtime. -If a client pulls a contract from Teleperformance, they don't let you go they just find another position for you in a different line of business.
cons: -As an advisor you can take no more than 6 days off other wise you have to go on LOA -As an advisor schedules are always changing so you have to be willing to change your schedule multiple times or just be willing to adapt to it. As a supervisor not as much. -As a supervisor you have to be willing to get new team members every 3 months as they progress to another department. So basically when you get your groove of coaching them and you start meeting your goals you have to start over with new people, so that can be challenging. -As a supervisor there is so much work that you have to really manage your time wisely, to get your work submitted in a timely manner. It can be overwhelming at times.
